description: Shanghai's iconic 1.5km waterfront promenade with 52 historic buildings in Gothic, Neo-Classical, Art Deco and other styles. Best viewed at night for the contrast between Puxi architecture and Pudong skyline.
One of China's first National First-Class Museums. Houses 1.02 million artifacts including 145,000 classified as national treasures. Bronze Gallery, Sculpture Gallery and Calligraphy masterpieces.
address: No. 201 Renmin Avenue, Huangpu District (People's Square Station, Metro Line 1/2/8)

description: Shanghai's 468m iconic tower with 11 futuristic spheres. Features 259m glass-bottomed observation deck, VR rollercoaster, and Shanghai History Museum recreating old Shikumen alleys.
address: No. 1 Century Avenue, Pudong District (Lujiazui Station, Metro Line 2)

description: China's No.1 Commercial Street. International brands, time-honored Chinese brands, duty-free goods. Tax refund available for departing international travelers.
address: East Nanjing Road, Huangpu District (Nanjing East Road Station, Metro Line 2/10)

description: Art district transformed from Shikumen (stone-gate) houses. Original designs, handmade jewelry, accessories, coffee-themed and cultural items. A blend of art galleries and unique boutique shops.
address: Lane 210, Taikang Road, Huangpu District (Dapuqiao Station, Metro Line 9)

description: International shopping district reimagined from Shikumen architecture. High-end retail stores, designer brands, imported home furnishings. Located adjacent to the First National Congress of the CPC.
address: Lane 181, Taicang Road, Huangpu District (Xintiandi Station, Metro Line 10/13)

description: A historic street preserving old Shanghai charm. Vintage stores, designer boutiques, Instagram-famous cafés. Home to the iconic Wukang Mansion. Top TikTok & REDnote check-in spot.
address: Wukang Road, Xuhui District (Jiaotong University Station, Metro Line 10/11)

description: Iconic triangular-shaped building in the French Concession. Famous for photography and history. A must-visit architectural landmark.
Open-air district blending nature with luxury. Luxury brands (Louis Vuitton, Dior), Tsutaya Books, concentration of international flagship stores. Rooftop garden.
address: Lane 500, Dongyu Road, Pudong New District (Dongfang Sports Center Station, Metro Line 6/8/11)

description: Luxury brands (Hermès, Bottega Veneta), organic supermarkets. Located adjacent to Jing'an Temple. Seamlessly blends commerce with culture. Hosts international art exhibitions.
address: 1515 West Nanjing Road, Jing'an District (Jing'an Temple Station, Metro Line 2/7)

description: Gothic Revival church built in 1869 by British architect Sir George Gilbert Scott. Served as the Anglican Church for British expatriates in Shanghai's international settlement era.
Neoclassical building built in 1918. Headquarters of Shen Bao, a famous newspaper that played an important role in Shanghai's modern journalism history. Now an office building.
Comprehensive ocean-themed amusement park. World's first Ultraman-themed pavilion, 155m underwater tunnel, Beluga Whale Theater. Night shows with light installations.
address: No. 1 Lingang Avenue, Pudong New District

description: Second teamLab Borderless museum in the world. 8,800sqm space with 50+ digital art works. 'Echoing Lamp Forest', 'Colorful Ocean Painting', and AI-generated 'Data Flower' installation.
address: No. 100, Huayuan Port Road, Huangpu District

description: World's largest astronomy museum. Spherical projection technology, films about The Big Bang and Black Holes. 'Metaverse Stargazing' project lets visitors roam the Milky Way with virtual astronauts via VR.
address: No. 380 Lingang Avenue, Pudong New Area

description: Industrial-style design venue. Hosts 'AI Art Season' with works like 'Flower of Data' and 'Mechanical Muse'. Rooftop café with Huangpu River views. Interactive mobile app exhibits.
address: No. 200, Huayuan Port Road, Huangpu District

description: 'Shanghai's Coolest Wet Market'. Fresh produce from local grannies, trendy bakeries, pop-up art exhibitions behind a retro-modern façade. Start your morning with a bowl of chaiban wontons.
address: No. 318 Urumqi Middle Road, Xuhui District

description: Shanghai's largest and most comprehensive market for plants, pets and antiques. 8,000sqm with 500-600 stalls. Vibrant roses, succulents, exotic reptiles, vintage porcelain and antiques.
address: No. 1539 Lingshi Road (Xincun Road Station, Metro Line 7)

description: Newly promoted Michelin one-star. Only restaurant in Shanghai to receive a star for its xiaolongbao. Signature 'Crab Trio' features clear amber broth with distinct crab meat grains. Open kitchen.
address: 103, No. 7 Maoming South Road, Huangpu District

description: Michelin Bib Gourmand 2025. Signature 'Pork Liver Noodles': pork liver stir-fried with hot oil for smoky aroma, served with alkaline noodles.

Shanghai's largest open-air night market. 'Intangible Cultural Heritage Food Area' added in 2025. Try Leshan bowl chicken with tripe soaked in red oil broth. Free power banks and bilingual maps at entrance.
24-hour store. The 'Four Great Staples' of Shanghai breakfast: salty soy milk with youtiao, tofu pudding. Late night: scallion-oil noodles. Vibrant atmosphere of old Shanghai.
Classic Shanghai sesame paste noodles, a local institution near Madang Road.

name.zh: OUR Bakery 中海环宇荟店

description: A restaurant located in Shanghai's Huangpu district.
Popular bakery chain with multiple Shanghai locations. Known for Japanese-style artisan breads.
address: 路269号 黄浦江购物中心1楼105号

description: A shopping located in Shanghai's Huangpu district.
Concept beauty and lifestyle store popular with younger Shanghai crowd.
A shopping located in Shanghai's Huangpu district.
Recommended butter bread in Shanghai. Good value compared to Korean or Japanese famous bakeries.
A restaurant located in Shanghai's Jing'an district.
address: 静安区 (70m walk from Exit 4, Nanjing West Road Metro Station)

description: French castle-style bakery. Famous matcha croissant with green tea aroma. Open 12:00-22:00.
address: 法式古堡面包坊 新天地店

description: Trending bakery collective. Known for matcha tiramisu croissant. Open Monday-Sunday.
Matcha beef croissant. Perfectly balanced tea flavor. Open 10:00-22:00.
address: 静安嘉里中心店 (Jing'an Kerry Centre)

description: Matcha puffs with high matcha content, ice cream-like cold filling. Open 10:00-22:00 (Mon-Sun).
address: 博华广场店 (near Natural History Museum)

description: One of Shanghai's 8 must-visit coffee shops. Known for quality specialty coffee.
Shanghai must-visit café. Known for specialty pour-over coffee.
Minimal, stylish café with quality coffee. One of Shanghai's 8 must-visit coffee shops.
Outdoor seating café in French Concession. Known for relaxed atmosphere.
Trendy café in French Concession area. Instagrammable storefront.
Manner Coffee branch with stunning floor-to-ceiling window view of Oriental Pearl Tower and The Bund across the river.
Unique themed café with warm, homey interior. Specialty pour-over.
Bookstore café hybrid. Surrounded by bookshelves with archway aesthetics. Great for reading and coffee.
Cozy café by the North Bund with views of Huangpu River and Pudong skyline. Popular free spot for expats and tourists.

name.zh: 苏州河边秋千咖啡 / oldsixcafeer

description: Charming riverside café with swings. Perfect for relaxing afternoons and photos along Suzhou Creek.
Affordable fashion market. Everything under RMB100. Multiple floors of budget clothing and accessories.
Full of local premium and niche brands. Youth-oriented shopping centre with international designer collaborations.
Tree-lined street full of cute boutique shops. Home to Harmay, 13DE MARZO, Wiggle Wiggle and many independent labels. One of Shanghai's most photographed streets.
Playful character-themed lifestyle shop on Anfu Road. Known for the giant bear mascot at the entrance.
Fashion boutique on Anfu Road. Bright, well-lit interiors. Popular with fashion-forward visitors.
Modern art museum near The Bund. Known for innovative exhibitions and cultural programming. Features river-facing galleries.
Preserved historical home of Sir He Dong, reflecting old Shanghai's elegance. Free to visit.
Picturesque flower shop on a street corner. Often featured in lifestyle photos and Xiaohongshu content.

name.en: Green House at Taiyuan & Yongjia Roads
name.zh: 太原路与永嘉路绿房子

description: Historic eye-catching building completely covered in green ivy, hidden in the French Concession. One of Shanghai's most photographed hidden gems.
address: Intersection of Taiyuan Road and Yongjia Road, French Concession

name.en: North Bund Mini Egg Dome Pathway
name.zh: 通往北外滩小巨蛋步道

description: Scenic walking path leading to the unique 'little egg-shaped' landmark along the Bund. Popular photography spot.
Shanghai's leading contemporary art hub in converted factories along Suzhou Creek. Galleries, studios, and independent concept stores.
Converted 1930s slaughterhouse now a creative space. Unique spiral walkways, pop-up exhibitions, concept stores, and events.
Youth-oriented art and lifestyle park. Outdoor terrace, restaurant clusters and concept stores. Across the river from Shanghai International Fashion Centre.
address: No. 535, Jinqiao Road, Pudong New District

description: Asia's largest glass greenhouse garden opened 2024. 'Dinosaur World', rain forest, 3000+ exotic plants. Open 09:00-17:00 (closed weekly on legal holidays except statutory holidays). Entry: ¥98/adult weekday, ¥128 weekend.
Along the Suzhou Creek riverside, open-air white bridge, outer blocks. 2km walkable. Good for night walks.
address: No. 100, Fujian North Road, Jing'an District

description: Urban renewal project in 1924 buildings including 萬屋書店 and former Navy Club (swimming pool). 1931 孙科别墅 (Sun Ke Villa). Concept stores, quality restaurants, outdoor terrace. Adjacent to many old Western-style villas.
Near Longhua Pagoda, Longhua Temple, Lie Shi Mausoleum. Good base for a full Xuhui citywalk day.
Two century-old Shikumen buildings (one was Yin Ruikeji church, now a gallery; one was originally a foreign concession patrol post, now a design co-working space). Creative dining recommendation.
Smoky warehouse-style Jiangnan alleyway market. Two floors with many types of shops. Good for browsing.
Known as 'Shanghai's No.1 Garden Street'. Tree-lined boulevard with 1920s-30s architecture, villas, and independent boutiques. Perfect for a relaxed stroll.
Branch of the world-famous photography museum. Rotating international exhibitions in a converted riverside building.
Creative art space and event venue in converted industrial buildings along Suzhou Creek.
Historic warehouse famous for the 1937 Battle of Shanghai. Now a memorial museum with original bullet hole markings on the exterior. Free entry.
address: Suzhou Creek north bank, Jing'an District

description: Historic lane complex on Sinan Road. Cluster of restored villas housing restaurants, boutiques, and Sinan Books. Weekend farmer's market.
Preserved former home of Dr. Sun Yat-sen, founder of modern China. Museum with original furnishings and historical exhibits.
Iconic independent bookstore in a historic villa on Sinan Road. Regular literary events and book signings.
French-style park in the heart of the former French Concession. Roses, plane trees, dancing locals, live music. One of Shanghai's most charming green spaces.
One of Shanghai's oldest synagogues, built 1920. A relic of the Jewish community that thrived in Shanghai. Guided tours available.
address: 陕西北路 (Shaanxi North Road), Jing'an District

description: Restored 1918 villa of the Rong family. Now a Prada-supported heritage space hosting exhibitions. Stunning European-style interiors.
Korean minimalist beauty and lifestyle brand. Known for beautifully curated store design.
address: Shaanxi North Road area, Jing'an District

description: Striking blue-domed Russian Orthodox church. Built for Shanghai's Russian émigré community. Now a cultural heritage site.
Small but charming park in the French Concession. Popular with elderly locals for morning exercises and afternoon relaxation.
Japanese-style concept lifestyle store. Known for curated selection of home goods, snacks, and small accessories.
Small, colourful lifestyle and gift shop. Instagram-popular for exterior signage.
Charming independent bookstore inside the historic Columbia Circle complex. Worth browsing.
Preserved 1931 villa of Sun Ke (son of Sun Yat-sen). Elegant colonial architecture inside the Columbia Circle complex.
address: Columbia Circle, Panyu Road, Changning District

description: Renovated Shikumen lane complex with concept cafés, art galleries, and lifestyle boutiques. One of Xinhua Road's hidden gems.
Peaceful garden with mature trees and fountain on Hengshan Road. Less visited than Fuxing Park but equally charming.
Popular bagel shop on Yongjia Road. New York-style bagels with various fillings. Regular queues.
Unique café and ceramics shop on Yongjia Road. Known for handmade pottery and specialty coffee.
Preserved former home of Xia Yan, celebrated Chinese screenwriter and cultural figure.

name.en: Zhou Gong Guan (Zhou Enlai's Former Residence)

description: Former residence and office of Zhou Enlai during the second KMT-CPC cooperation period. Free museum with period furnishings.
Historic complex on Hengshan Road with bars, restaurants and the iconic Hengshan bar street. Evening entertainment hub.
Multi-floor Korean shopping mall with glass skylight ceiling. Korean restaurants, fashion, beauty and food stalls. Unique indoor shopping experience.
Specialty coffee shop inside the Columbia Circle complex. Popular with the creative crowd.
Trendy Shanghai bakery known for creative flavour combinations and quality croissants.
Shanghai's oldest and largest Buddhist temple. 7-storey pagoda dating to the 3rd century CE. Cherry blossoms in spring.

name.zh: 哈氏上海哈尔滨食品厂

description: Shanghai institution selling classic Harbin-style cakes, biscuits and confections since the 1950s. Queue for the red bean cake.
address: Madang Road / Xintiandi area, Huangpu District

description: Asia's largest zoo covering 740,000 sqm with over 6,000 animals including 600+ rare species. Giant pandas, golden monkeys, snow leopards. Panda house and children's zoo are highlights.
address: No. 2381 Hongqiao Road, Changning District (Hongqiao Road Station, Metro Line 10)

description: World-class theme park with 7 uniquely themed lands. TRON Lightcycle Power Run roller coaster, Pirates of the Caribbean: Battle for the Sunken Treasure, and Zootopia Land (opened 2023). Parades and nightly fireworks shows.
address: No. 310 Huangzhao Road, Chuansha Town, Pudong New Area (Disney Station, Metro Line 11)

description: China's first LEGOLAND Resort, second-largest globally. Opened July 2025. 8 themed areas, 75+ interactive attractions. LEGO City, NINJAGO World, LEGO Kingdoms. 1:1 LEGO brick replicas of global landmarks.
address: No. 999, Lebin Road, Fengjing Town, Jinshan District (1km from Jinshan North Railway Station)

description: New flagship branch of Shanghai Museum opened 2025. 13 permanent galleries with 145,000+ rare treasures. World's largest ancient Chinese art museum. Capacity 20,000 visitors/day. Free entry.
address: No. 1952 Century Avenue, Pudong New Area (Shanghai Science & Technology Museum Station, Metro Line 2/4/6)

description: World's largest astronomy museum. Spherical projection technology, 8K dome theater, VR Milky Way experience. Films about The Big Bang and Black Holes. Summer 2025 evening sessions on Saturdays 17:00–20:00.
address: No. 380 Lingang Avenue, Pudong New Area (Dishui Lake Station, Metro Line 16, then 10-min walk)

description: Largest water park in eastern China, 128,000 sqm. Wave pools, lazy river, extreme slides, sandy beach. Seasonal — open late June to early September only. Beach concerts and evening events.
address: No. 888, Linhu Road, Sheshan Town, Songjiang District (Sheshan Station, Metro Line 9 + free shuttle)

description: One of China's top science museums with 14 permanent exhibitions. IMAX theater, biodiversity hall, robotics and space exploration galleries. Interactive exhibits for all ages.
address: No. 2000 Century Avenue, Pudong New Area (Shanghai Science & Technology Museum Station, Metro Line 2)

description: Asia's largest glass greenhouse. 3,000+ exotic plant species, rain forest dome, Dinosaur World, and butterfly garden. Opened 2024. Stunning architectural glass structure.

1,500-year-old water town known as 'Home of Chinese Folk Painting'. 52 bridges, Ming and Qing dynasty architecture, whitewashed walls and black-tile roofs. Birthplace of Jinshan peasant painting. One of the four most famous towns in south China.
address: 28 Alley 8588, Tingfeng Road, Fengjing Town, Jinshan District (57km from city center)

description: China's oldest surviving all-steel riveted bridge, opened 1908. 106.7m camelback truss design. Connects the north end of The Bund to Hongkou. WWII landmark. Declared Heritage Architecture 1994. Best photographed at dusk.
address: Junction of Suzhou Creek and Huangpu River, Huangpu District (connecting Huangpu and Hongkou)

description: 800-year-old Jiangnan water town in Pudong. 68 ornate gateways, 2km old street, 4 intersecting waterways, 20+ stone bridges. Less touristy than Zhujiajiao. Home to national intangible heritage: luogu shu storytelling and Pudong pipa school. 2025 World Heritage application.
address: No. 128 Haiquan Street, Pudong New Area (36km from city center, near Pudong Airport)

description: 12.5km public waterfront pathway along Suzhou Creek. Walk, run, or cycle past M50 Art District, Sihang Warehouse, Fotografiska, 1933 Old Millfun, and riverside cafés. Blend of historic warehouses and contemporary art spaces. Far less crowded than The Bund.
address: 12.5km riverside pathway from Waibaidu Bridge to Shanghai Central Station (multiple Metro access points)
